tasks.title: Architecture cleanup: remove dead code and fix naming conflicts
acceptance_criteria: harness/embed.py::dense_retrieve() removed — identical functionality exists in retrieve.py::retrieve(mode='dense')
harness/agents/react_agent.py has a module-level deprecation notice pointing to harness/chains/agents/react.py
All output dicts in harness/chains/ use 'prompt_strategy' key instead of 'strategy' to prevent confusion with retrieval strategy
harness/chains/agents/crag.py updated: output key renamed to prompt_strategy
harness/chains/agents/react.py updated: output key renamed to prompt_strategy
harness/chains/simple_rag.py updated: output key renamed to prompt_strategy
pytest tests/test_langchain_chains.py passes (update test assertions that check the key name)
grep -r '"strategy"' harness/chains/ shows no remaining 'strategy' keys in output dicts

tasks.title: Retrieval config schema and unified retriever factory
acceptance_criteria: RetrievalConfig dataclass defined in harness/retrieve.py with fields: mode, strategy, k, recursive_max_hops, hierarchical_summary_dir
make_retriever(config, index) factory in harness/retrieve.py routes to the correct EMARetriever subclass based on config.strategy
EMARetriever in harness/chains/retriever.py updated to accept strategy='flat'|'recursive'|'hierarchical' parameter (defaults to 'flat' — backward compatible)
run_eval.py uses make_retriever(cfg, index) instead of the bespoke retrieve_fn closure (ablation steps A1/A2/A3 remain as post-processing wrappers around make_retriever)
app.py retrieval step uses EMARetriever with configurable mode (removes direct retrieve() import)
All existing harness/configs/*.yaml files run unchanged (flat strategy is default if key absent)
YAML config extended: retrieval.strategy key documented in baseline_a0.yaml
pytest tests/ passes with no regressions

tasks.title: Recursive retrieval strategy: automatic cross_ref expansion
acceptance_criteria: RecursiveEMARetriever (or EMARetriever with strategy='recursive') performs initial flat retrieval then expands via cross_refs metadata
Expansion is limited by config.recursive_max_hops (default 1); max_hops=0 degenerates to flat
Expanded nodes are deduplicated and appended after initial results (so top-k initial results are preserved at the front)
Config key: retrieval.strategy: recursive with optional retrieval.recursive.max_hops: 1
New config file: harness/configs/retrieval_recursive.yaml with run_id: recursive_hybrid, mode: hybrid, strategy: recursive

Tests in tests/test_retrieval_strategies.py: (1) corpus with cross_refs → expanded results include referenced nodes; (2) max_hops=0 → no expansion; (3) circular cross_ref → no infinite loop; (4) no cross_refs → identical to flat
make_retriever(config, index) correctly instantiates recursive retriever when strategy='recursive'

tasks.title: Hierarchical DocumentSummaryIndex: EMA page-level summary nodes
acceptance_criteria: build_hierarchical_index(corpus_path, index_dir, *, force=False, embed_model=None) in harness/embed.py (or harness/embed_hierarchical.py) creates a two-level node structure
Parent nodes: one per unique source_url, text = 'Title: {source_title}\nTopic: {topic_path}\nQ&A count: {n}'; metadata includes source_url, source_type, topic_path, child_qa_ids list
Child nodes: existing Q&A TextNodes (unchanged) with PARENT relationship pointing to the parent node ID
Hierarchical index persisted alongside flat index (new subdir: hierarchical/) so both can coexist

HierarchicalEMARetriever (or EMARetriever with strategy='hierarchical'): retrieves top parent nodes by dense similarity, expands to all child Q&A nodes for each matched parent, re-ranks children by dense similarity to original query
Config: retrieval.strategy: hierarchical, retrieval.hierarchical.top_doc_k: 5 (number of parent docs to expand)
New config file: harness/configs/retrieval_hierarchical.yaml
Tests: corpus of 2 source_urls × 3 Q&As each; retrieval of a targeted query returns Q&As from the correct source_url parent
build_hierarchical_index does NOT require a new LLM call — parent summary is constructed from metadata, not LLM-generated

tasks.title: Adaptive strategy selection from rated trajectories
acceptance_criteria: CacheEntry dataclass in harness/query_cache.py gains two new nullable fields: retrieval_strategy (str|None) and question_type (str|None='T1'|'T2'|'T3'|'T4')
Backward compatible: existing cache JSON files load correctly (fields default to None when absent)
StrategySelector class in harness/strategy_selector.py with method suggest(query_vec, cache, *, default='flat') -> str
suggest() finds similar past questions (similarity >= 0.85), groups by retrieval_strategy, computes weighted-average rating (weight = similarity), returns highest-scoring strategy
Falls back to default when: cache is empty, no rated entries, no entries with retrieval_strategy set, or best strategy has fewer than 3 supporting examples
StrategySelector.explain(query_vec, cache) -> dict returns the rationale (strategy, supporting_examples, avg_rating) for transparency/debugging
app.py calls StrategySelector.suggest() and displays the recommended strategy name in the retrieval step header
Cache.add_entry() signature extended: add retrieval_strategy=None, question_type=None parameters

Tests: (1) empty cache → fallback; (2) 5 rated entries all using 'recursive' with rating 5 → suggest returns 'recursive'; (3) mixed strategies → highest-rated wins; (4) below similarity threshold → fallback

tasks.title: Eval harness and chain registry integration
acceptance_criteria: All harness/configs/*.yaml files include retrieval.strategy key (add 'flat' as explicit default to existing files)
run_langsmith_eval.py uses make_retriever(cfg, index) from retrieve.py — consistent with run_eval.py
CHAIN_REGISTRY in harness/chains/registry.py updated: builder functions accept and forward strategy from retriever config
Two new registry entries added as examples: 'recursive_cot' (recursive retriever + cot_self prompt) and 'hierarchical_zero' (hierarchical retriever + zero_shot prompt)
harness/configs/retrieval_recursive.yaml and retrieval_hierarchical.yaml run end-to-end with run_eval.py (benchmark path can be missing — just checks config parsing and retriever init)
python3 -m harness.chains.registry --list shows at least 7 chains including the two new ones
pytest tests/ passes with no regressions

tasks.title: Documentation: retrieval strategy guide and architecture update
acceptance_criteria: docs/retrieval_strategies.md created with: (1) overview of the strategy layer; (2) config reference table for each strategy; (3) per-question-type guidance (T1-T4 which strategy to try first); (4) ASCII architecture diagram; (5) step-by-step guide to adding a new strategy; (6) known limitations and tradeoffs

DECISIONS.md updated with entry: 'Retrieval strategy layer — 2026-05-22' explaining the RetrievalConfig factory, naming clarification (retrieval strategy vs prompt strategy), and the decision to keep strategies composable with prompting variants
OPEN_QUESTIONS.md updated: add question about whether hierarchical index summary quality affects T2/T4 and when to switch to LLM-generated summaries
README (if exists) or CLAUDE.md retrieval section updated to mention strategy config key
.claude/HISTORY.md row appended for this work unit
